intitule: Would Rather and Had Better: Identification

enonce.texte:
Exercise 1: Identify the Expression

Read the following sentences and identify whether they use would rather (preference) or had better (strong advice/warning).

questions.texte: I would rather stay at home than go to the party.
questions.reponse: Would rather (preference)

questions.texte: You had better take an umbrella. It looks like rain.
questions.reponse: Had better (strong advice/warning)

questions.texte: She would rather study medicine than law.
questions.reponse: Would rather (preference)

questions.texte: We had better leave now or we'll be late.
questions.reponse: Had better (strong advice/warning)

questions.texte: They would rather eat at home than go to a restaurant.
questions.reponse: Would rather (preference)

enonce.texte:
Exercise 2: Expressing Preference

Rewrite the following sentences using would rather. Make any necessary changes.

questions.texte: I prefer tea to coffee.
questions.reponse: I would rather have tea than coffee.

questions.texte: She prefers to walk to school instead of taking the bus.
questions.reponse: She would rather walk to school than take the bus.

questions.texte: They prefer watching movies at home.
questions.reponse: They would rather watch movies at home.

questions.texte: He prefers to study alone rather than in a group.
questions.reponse: He would rather study alone than in a group.

questions.texte: We prefer not to go out tonight.
questions.reponse: We would rather not go out tonight.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 3: Giving Strong Advice

Rewrite the following sentences using had better to express strong advice or warning.

questions.texte: It is raining heavily. You should take an umbrella.
questions.reponse: You had better take an umbrella.

questions.texte: She must study for the exam if she wants to pass.
questions.reponse: She had better study for the exam if she wants to pass.

questions.texte: It's getting late. We should leave now.
questions.reponse: We had better leave now.

questions.texte: The traffic is terrible. You should take the train.
questions.reponse: You had better take the train.

questions.texte: He is very sick. He should see a doctor.
questions.reponse: He had better see a doctor.

intitule: Would Rather and Had Better: Fill in the Blanks

enonce.texte:
Exercise 4: Choose the Correct Expression

Complete the sentences with would rather or had better in the correct form.

questions.texte: I __________ (to stay) at home tonight than go out.
questions.reponse: would rather stay

questions.texte: You __________ (to finish) your homework before you go out.
questions.reponse: had better finish

questions.texte: She __________ (not to go) to the party if she is tired.
questions.reponse: would rather not go

questions.texte: We __________ (to leave) early tomorrow to avoid traffic.
questions.reponse: had better leave

questions.texte: They __________ (to eat) at home than at a restaurant.
questions.reponse: would rather eat

intitule: Would Rather and Had Better in Context

enonce.texte:
Exercise 5: Real-Life Situations

Read the following situations and write a sentence using would rather or had better accordingly.

questions.texte: You are at a restaurant and you have a choice between fish and chicken. You prefer fish.
questions.reponse: I would rather have fish than chicken.

questions.texte: Your friend is driving very fast on a dangerous road. You want to warn him.
questions.reponse: You had better slow down. It's dangerous.

questions.texte: You are tired and you have to choose between going to the gym or staying home.
questions.reponse: I would rather stay home than go to the gym.

questions.texte: Your sister is going out without a jacket. You warn her because it's cold outside.
questions.reponse: You had better take a jacket. It's cold outside.

questions.texte: You have a choice between taking the bus or walking to school. You prefer walking.
questions.reponse: I would rather walk to school than take the bus.

intitule: Would Rather and Had Better: Error Correction

enonce.texte:
Exercise 6: Find and Correct the Mistakes

Each sentence contains a mistake with would rather or had better. Find the mistake and correct it.

questions.texte: I would rather to stay home than go out.
questions.reponse: I would rather stay home than go out. (remove 'to')

questions.texte: You had better to take your medicine now.
questions.reponse: You had better take your medicine now. (remove 'to')

questions.texte: She would rather not going to the party.
questions.reponse: She would rather not go to the party. (use base form 'go')

questions.texte: We had better not to be late for the meeting.
questions.reponse: We had better not be late for the meeting. (remove 'to')

questions.texte: They would rather not to eat that food.
questions.reponse: They would rather not eat that food. (remove 'to')
